context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Left

episode_text:
day=22; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Left; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit, dessert, or another cold-served item AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold items were explicitly preferred without reheating across contexts. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken breast strips, or hash browns THEN 2 min or 3 min BECAUSE personal hot-meal evidence shows a stable preference for reheating, with both stronger and slightly moderated reheating observed in quiet personal settings. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, potatoes, hash browns, chicken nuggets, or General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 3 min BECAUSE laptop hot-meal evidence repeatedly favored substantial reheating. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV, including when the TV is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, hash browns, bite-sized sandwiches, pizza, brownies with sauce, or fish bites with potatoes THEN 3 min BECAUSE TV meals consistently show a strong preference for thoroughly reheated food. IF dining context is social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken nuggets, chicken breast strips, hash browns, broccoli, ketchup, or General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated social-meal evidence shows a moderated reheating preference relative to the stronger 3 min pattern in solitary and screen-focused contexts.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a front-facing focal activity such as watching TV, working on a laptop, or interacting with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred preserving the front view in front-focused contexts, and the new laptop-left meal still used front protection, suggesting the main concern is the screen/workspace rather than the side location of the device. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the right such as watching TV on the right, working on a laptop on the right, or eating socially with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly selected preserving the right-side view across multiple right-focused contexts. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the left such as watching TV on the left or eating socially with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE left-focused evidence includes both entertainment and social contexts, supporting protection of the left-side view when attention or companionship is there. IF dining context is personal with no salient external focal target AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated personal meals support the pattern that occlusion concerns are mainly tied to protecting a specific line of sight. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a simple cold meal such as fruit THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE one observed social-front fruit meal relaxed the usual front-protection pattern. Across observed evidence, the tie-break is to protect the most behaviorally important line of sight, with front protection favored in work/screen contexts and side protection favored when the salient partner or display is clearly lateral.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN slow BECAUSE the user has usually selected the slower movement option, fitting a comfort-first interaction style, and the new laptop-left hot meal is consistent with that pattern. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a simple cold fruit meal THEN medium BECAUSE one laptop-fruit episode showed a modest increase in speed, consistent with tolerating somewhat faster motion for a straightforward meal while occupied. Across observed evidence, the tie-break remains comfort-first, with medium appearing as a narrow efficiency adjustment rather than a global shift.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition such as fruit pieces, strawberries, broccoli, brownies, fish bites, or other compact hot solid foods AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused work contexts, or TV contexts THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-acquisition-success option across several feeding meals. IF food is a long, narrow, elongated, nugget-like, strip-like, wedge-like, or piecewise hand-food item such as chicken strips, chicken breast strips, bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, General Tso's chicken pieces, or bite-sized pizza AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, entertainment-focused, social, or task-focused THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated selections with elongated or discrete hand-food-style items, now including a laptop chicken-nugget meal, indicate that food shape can override the general preference for acquisition success. Across observed evidence, food geometry is the main driver of this preference.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food, including simple fruit meals and substantial hot meals, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted explicit web confirmation in social settings, indicating a reliability preference despite extra interaction time. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a straightforward hot finger-food-style or bite-sized meal such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken breast strips, or hash browns THEN yes or no BECAUSE personal evidence shows both skipping confirmation to reduce interaction burden and requesting confirmation to ensure reliable bite acquisition for similar hot meals. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left, or entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is straightforward fork-fed food, including strawberries, mixed fruit, brownies, bananas, watermelon, cantaloupe, bite-sized pizza, chicken nuggets, or other simple hot solids, THEN no BECAUSE the user often skipped confirmation in divided-attention contexts to reduce interruption for simpler foods. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a more substantial hot savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN yes BECAUSE laptop-work hot-meal evidence shows that reliability can override interruption reduction for heavier meals. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more substantial or messier hot fork-fed meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or fish bites with potatoes and tartar sauce THEN yes BECAUSE observed TV evidence indicates that for some heavier hot meals the user favors reliability over reduced interaction. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is reliability in social settings and for heavier or less certain hot meals, but lower interaction burden in divided-attention settings for simpler foods; personal hot meals remain mixed rather than governed by a single fixed rule.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward due to limited trunk control, and they consistently selected inside-mouth transfer across observed meals.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user consistently selected inside-mouth transfer and their physical profile supports that mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a nonverbal readiness cue in social settings, consistent with reducing conversational disruption and unnecessary audio. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE work-context evidence consistently favors quiet visual signaling. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a substantial hot meal such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken breast strips, or hash browns THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal hot-meal evidence shows a preference for a more salient multimodal cue in a quiet solitary setting. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN speech or speech + LED BECAUSE TV episodes show the user often wants more salient cues while attention is occupied by the screen, including speech-only in one right-TV meal. Across observed evidence, the stable tie-break is quiet visual signaling in social and work contexts, with audio salience added in personal and screen-focused contexts.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND dining context is personal or social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and has selected open-mouth initiation in non-screen settings, favoring explicit user-driven readiness over automatic progression when not distracted by a screen. IF feeding task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred more automation when attention was occupied, including in the new laptop-left meal. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is toward automation under divided attention, while non-screen personal and social settings favor explicit but still low-effort open-mouth signaling.

IF drinking task AND dining context is personal or social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for drinking in observed non-screen meals, including social settings. IF drinking task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided, including in the new laptop-left meal. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is explicit low-effort initiation in non-screen settings and more automation in divided-attention settings.

IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is personal or social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for wiping in observed non-screen meals, including social settings. IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided, including in the new laptop-left meal. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is explicit low-effort initiation in non-screen settings and more automation in divided-attention settings.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a nonverbal cue in social settings when the tool reached transfer position, suggesting a stable preference for quiet completion signaling. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE work-context evidence consistently favors quiet visual-only completion signaling. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a substantial hot meal such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken breast strips, or hash browns THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal hot-meal evidence shows that the user may want a more salient multimodal completion cue in a quiet solitary setting. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN speech or speech + LED BECAUSE TV episodes show the user often wants more salient completion cues while attention is occupied by the screen, including speech-only in one right-TV meal. Across observed evidence, LED remains the stable non-TV social/work baseline, with speech-based salience increases in personal and screen-focused contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any context THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, which fits their inability to reliably press buttons.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for drinking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for wiping.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person or focal activity that the user wants to keep visible, including in front, to the right, or to the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reducing prolonged visual obstruction in social, laptop-working, and TV contexts, and the new laptop-left meal reinforces that visibility protection generalizes across work contexts as well. IF dining context is personal with no salient external focal target AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN no BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user is willing to skip retracting to reduce meal time when view obstruction is less important. Across observed evidence, the tie-break is to retract when preserving visibility matters, but to skip retracting in quiet personal meals to improve efficiency.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE observed personal evidence with ranch dressing shows the user prefers some dipping but not heavy coating in a quiet solitary setting. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred limited dipping in social meals, consistent with reducing messiness around other people. IF food includes a dip or sauce or topping AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping or extra coating in observed laptop meals, including a ketchup meal while working, suggesting a strong mess-avoidance preference when occupied. IF food includes a dip or sauce or topping AND dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed TV meals were explicitly set to no dipping, extending the pattern that divided-attention contexts favor minimizing mess. IF food is a sauced hot meal where dipping is unnecessary, such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli, OR a hot meal without a separate dip such as chicken breast strips and hash browns, AND dining context is personal or social AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no additional dipping when sauce was already part of the meal or when no separate dip was used. IF food has no dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable and observed no-sauce meals also used do-not-dip.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec or 1000 sec BECAUSE personal meals show both a very long intervention window and a moderate one, indicating that quiet personal settings can support either extra response time or somewhat faster progression. IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a moderate wait in social meals, balancing efficiency with enough time to respond using assistive interaction methods. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 1000 sec for strongly attention-capturing work contexts, but 100 sec has also been observed for a simpler cold-fruit laptop meal BECAUSE the user usually prefers a long intervention window while working, yet some straightforward meals tolerated a shorter moderate wait. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec or 1000 sec BECAUSE TV meals show both a moderate and a very long wait preference. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is a moderate 100 sec in social and some personal or TV meals, with 1000 sec retained when the user likely wants a larger intervention window due to distraction or slower response.
